reflects some of the underlying
messages from the joint OPC IPCA
report and the Tamifuna decision,
which is to say that
the bill really only deals with half the problem.
It deals with the point of collection of information by police.
But the messages from the joint report and from
Tamifuna were really saying that you have to consider
intelligence gathering within the full life cycle of the
use of that information and its retention.
And it's in this increasingly intensified
technological progress,
where that information is going into network databases
for analysis and insight, and so on.
So really, the message from the Tamifuna court, and if you think
about what His Honor Justice Koch was saying,
he was really inviting Parliament to create a clear
framework, not just about the authorization for police, but
about retention, use, and destruction of information.
And so
our perspective is that without addressing that side
of the equation, which is useful for the system,
it's useful for police to provide greater certainty.
Without that part of the equation, the safeguards, there's
likely still to be scope for
information gathering to be challenged within the courts, and for the
courts to have a look at that under the privacy
settings which are embedded legally within the Privacy Act, the
Search and Surveillance Act, and the New Zealand Bill of Rights Act.
All right. Hey, we have to bring it to a close.
But thank you for your submission, a weighty submission.
There's obviously Todd asked some specific stuff, and I've
asked for some other stuff. We'd appreciate if you were able to
help the committees do deliberations around those aspects.
So if you could come back to us, that'd be very helpful.
But I want to thank you all for coming through this morning and
giving your thoughts.
Thank you, Mr. Chair. Thank you, members.
Yes, if there are any further questions, very happy
to respond to them through your advisors to the committee.
And at least we haven't covered everything that was asked by Ms.
Bellich.
Okay. All right, thank you. Now, members, we're going to go into private
session just briefly, if you want to transfer.
Thank you, everyone.
